task_completion.description: Create a waypoint named 'Damaged Bridge B-42'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a waypoint named 'Damaged Bridge B-42' in the application's list view, map view, or properties dialog.

task_completion.description: Set the waypoint coordinates to N 42.45100°, W 71.09800°
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the waypoint properties dialog showing the position/coordinates set to approximately N 42.45100° and W 71.09800°.

task_completion.description: Add a web link with the specified URL and description
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Links tab in the waypoint properties showing the URL 'https://city.gov/engineering/reports/b42.pdf' and the description 'Structural Assessment B-42'.

task_completion.description: Assign the waypoint to a custom category named 'Maintenance_Priority_1'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Categories tab or section in the waypoint properties showing 'Maintenance_Priority_1' created and checked/assigned to this waypoint.

task_completion.description: Export the data as a GPX file to C:\workspace\output\maintenance_export.gpx
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog, save as dialog, or file explorer confirming the file 'maintenance_export.gpx' is being saved to or exists in 'C:\workspace\output'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Garmin BaseCamp GUI to create and modify the waypoint.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Garmin BaseCamp interface and its waypoint properties dialogs, rather than a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the BaseCamp 'Export' dialog being used to save the GPX file, rather than a script or manual file creation.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type the GPX XML content into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of Notepad, VS Code, or other text editors containing raw GPX/XML data.
